38{
39
40 /* here it computes the size of required memory
41 and returns the number of allocatable particles for fragmentation
42
43 ZELDOVICH DISPLACEMENTS:
44
45 name sizeof N
46
47 products 4 float + 1 int = 20 MyGrids[0].total_local_size
48 kdensity 1 double = 8 MyGrids[*].total_local_size_fft
49 second_derivatives 6 double = 48 MyGrids[*].total_local_size
50
51 first_derivatives and density will share the same memory as second_derivatives
52
53 these will be allocated by pfft routines:
54
55 cvector_fft 1 double = 8 MyGrids[*].total_local_size_fft
56 rvector_fft 1 double = 8 MyGrids[*].total_local_size_fft
57
58 This is needed by GenIC:
59 seedtable 1 int = 4 MyGrids[*].GSglobal[_x_] * MyGrids[*].GSglobal[_y_]
60
61 Total for ZEL: 76 + 16 = 92 + overhead for fragmentation + smaller things (seedtable etc)
62
63 2LPT/3LPT DISPLACEMENTS
64
65 name sizeof N
66
67 products (2LPT): 7 float + 1 int = 32 MyGrids[0].total_local_size
68 products (3LPT) 13 float + 1 int = 56 MyGrids[0].total_local_size
69 kdensity 1 double = 8 MyGrids[*].total_local_size_fft
70 second_derivatives 6 double = 48 MyGrids[*].total_local_size
71 kvector_2LPT 1 double = 8 MyGrids[0].total_local_size_fft
72 kvector_3LPT_1 1 double = 8 MyGrids[0].total_local_size_fft
73 kvector_3LPT_2 1 double = 8 MyGrids[0].total_local_size_fft
74
75 first_derivatives and density will share the same memory as second_derivatives
76 source_?LPT* will share the same memory as kvector_?LPT*
77
78 This is needed by GenIC:
79 seedtable 1 int = 4 MyGrids[*].GSglobal[_x_] * MyGrids[*].GSglobal[_y_]
80
81 These will be allocated by pfft routines:
82
83 cvector_fft 1 double = 8 MyGrids[*].total_local_size_fft
84 rvector_fft 1 double = 8 MyGrids[*].total_local_size_fft
85
86 Total for 2LPT: 32 + 80 + 16 = 128 + overhead for fragmentation + smaller things
87 Total for 3LPT: 56 + 80 + 16 = 152 + overhead for fragmentation + smaller things
88
89 Timeless snapshot adds 4 bytes to the count.
90
91 In case or recomputation of displacements:
92
93 products (ZEL): 7 float + 1 int = 32 MyGrids[0].total_local_size
94 products (2LPT): 13 float + 1 int = 56 MyGrids[0].total_local_size
95 products (3LPT): 25 float + 1 int = 104 MyGrids[0].total_local_size
96 Total for ZEL: 32 + 80 + 16 = 128 + overhead for fragmentation
97 Total for 2LPT: 56 + 80 + 16 = 152 + overhead for fragmentation
98 Total for 3LPT: 104 + 80 + 16 = 200 + overhead for fragmentation
99
100 Products for fragmentation will require the same memory as those for fmax
101 plus the overhead for boundary layers.
102
103 Group catalogs will require less memory than the fmax products. This will be
104 checked with an estimate of the number of halos.
105
106 memory.prods: fmax products in fft space
107 memory.fields_to_keep LPT sources in kspace
108 memory.fields second_derivatives, // seedtable
109 memory.first_allocated: the three above
110 memory.fft: needed by pfft vectors
111 memory.fmax_total: needed by fmax, the sum of fft and first_allocated
112 memory.frag_prods: fmax products in subbox space
113 memory.frag_arrays: linking list and group ID
114 memory.groups: groups catalog, including histories, map and PLC
115 memory.frag_allocated: needed by fragment, allocated
116 memory.frag_total: needed by fragment, including FFTs
117 memory.all_allocated: total allocated memory (not including FFTs)
118 memory.all: total memory needed (including FFTs)
119
120 */
121
122 int igrid;
123 unsigned int myNalloc, Nalloc;
124
125 /***********************************************/
126 /* COLLAPSE TIMES PART */
127 /* this is the memory needed to store products */
128 memory.prods = MyGrids[0].total_local_size * sizeof(product_data); /* products */
129 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(memory.prods);
130 memory.fields = memory.fields_to_keep = 0;
131
132 /* these fields are needed after products if displacements are recomputed */
133 for (igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
134 {
135 memory.fields_to_keep += MyGrids[igrid].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double); /* kdensity */
136 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(memory.fields_to_keep);
137 }
138#ifdef TWO_LPT
139 memory.fields_to_keep += MyGrids[0].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double); /* kvector_2LPT */
140 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(memory.fields_to_keep);
141#ifdef THREE_LPT
142 memory.fields_to_keep += MyGrids[0].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double); /* kvector_3LPT_1 */
143 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(memory.fields_to_keep);
144 memory.fields_to_keep += MyGrids[0].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double); /* kvector_3LPT_2 */
145 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(memory.fields_to_keep);
146#endif
147#endif
148
149 /* these fields are needed only by Fmax */
150 for (igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
151 for (int dim = 0; dim < 6; dim++)
152 {
153 memory.fields += MyGrids[igrid].total_local_size * sizeof(double); /* second_derivatives */
154 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(memory.fields);
155 }
156
157 /* for (igrid=0; igrid<Ngrids; igrid++) */
158 /* memory.fields += MyGrids[igrid].GSglobal[_x_] * MyGrids[igrid].GSglobal[_y_] * sizeof(unsigned int); /\* seedtable *\/ */
159
160 /* memory allocated by PFFT */
161 for (igrid = 0, memory.fft = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
162 {
163 memory.fft += MyGrids[igrid].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double);
164 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(memory.fft);
165 memory.fft += MyGrids[igrid].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double);
166 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(memory.fft);
167 }
168
169 memory.first_allocated = memory.prods + memory.fields_to_keep + memory.fields;
170 memory.fmax_total = memory.first_allocated + memory.fft;
171
172 /********************************************************************************/
173 /* FRAGMENTATION PART */
174 /* this is the memory needed to fragment the collapsed medium, including PLC data
175 and the map for needed particles on the boundary */
176 memory.groups = subbox.PredNpeaks * sizeof(group_data);
177 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(memory.groups);
178 memory.groups += subbox.PredNpeaks * +sizeof(histories_data);
179 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(memory.groups);
180#ifdef PLC
181 memory.groups += plc.Nmax * sizeof(plcgroup_data);
182 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(memory.groups);
183#endif
184 memory.groups += subbox.maplength * sizeof(unsigned int);
185 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(memory.groups);
186 memory.groups += subbox.maplength * sizeof(unsigned int);
187 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(memory.groups);
188
189 /* Here it computes the number of particles it can allocate for the subbox */
190 size_t other_mem = memory.prods + memory.groups
191#ifdef RECOMPUTE_DISPLACEMENTS
192 + memory.fields_to_keep + memory.fft
193#endif
194 ;
195
196 if (other_mem > MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask * params.MaxMemPerParticle)
197 myNalloc = 0;
198 else
199 /* -10 is to compensate for remainder in integer division */
200 myNalloc = (MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask * params.MaxMemPerParticle - other_mem) / (sizeof(product_data) + FRAGFIELDS * sizeof(int)) - 10;
201
202 /* Nalloc will be the smallest among all tasks */
203 MPI_Reduce(&myNalloc, &Nalloc, 1, MPI_UNSIGNED, MPI_MIN, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
204 MPI_Bcast(&Nalloc, 1, MPI_UNSIGNED, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
205
206 memory.frag_prods = Nalloc * sizeof(product_data);
207 memory.frag_arrays = Nalloc * FRAGFIELDS * sizeof(int);
208 /* this is the memory occupied by fragmentation */
209 memory.frag_allocated = memory.prods + memory.frag_prods + memory.groups + memory.frag_arrays
210#ifdef RECOMPUTE_DISPLACEMENTS
211 + memory.fields_to_keep
212#endif
213 ;
214 memory.frag_total = memory.frag_allocated
215#ifdef RECOMPUTE_DISPLACEMENTS
216 + memory.fft
217#endif
218 ;
219
220#ifdef CLASSIC_FRAGMENTATION
221 if (myNalloc < subbox.Npart)
222 {
223 printf("ERROR: Task %d can allocate only %d subbox particles, while %d are needed;\n",
224 ThisTask, myNalloc, subbox.Npart);
225 printf(" a large overhead is probably needed, please increase MaxMemPerParticle\n");
226 return (size_t)0;
227 }
228#endif
229
230 /* this is the largest amount of memory needed by the code */
231 memory.all_allocated = (memory.first_allocated > memory.frag_allocated ? memory.first_allocated : memory.frag_allocated);
232 memory.all = (memory.fmax_total > memory.frag_total ? memory.fmax_total : memory.frag_total);
233
234 double myfraction = (double)Nalloc / (double)subbox.Ngood;
235 double minfraction;
236 MPI_Reduce(&myfraction, &minfraction, 1, MPI_DOUBLE, MPI_MIN, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
237
238 if (!ThisTask)
239 {
240 if (minfraction < 0.5)
241 {
242 printf("ERROR: for some tasks the number of allocatable particles is only a factor %8f\n", minfraction);
243 printf(" times the number of good particles in the subbox; this is far too small!\n");
244 printf(" Please increase MaxMemPerParticle\n");
245 fflush(stdout);
246 return (size_t)0;
247 }
248 }
249
250 return Nalloc;
251}
252
253int allocate_main_memory()
254{
255 /*
256 This routine allocates all the memory needed to contain information
257 on particles and groups.
258 */
259
260 int igrid, i;
261 size_t count_memory;
262 struct map
263 {
264 int lx, ly, lz;
265 double oh, am, m1, m2, m3, m4, m5, m6, m7, m8;
266 } mymap;
267 MPI_Status status;
268#ifdef VERBOSE
269 size_t *bcast;
270 int bsize, n;
271 void *last;
272#endif
273
274 /* map of memory usage for all tasks */
275 mymap.lx = MyGrids[0].GSlocal[_x_];
276 mymap.ly = MyGrids[0].GSlocal[_y_];
277 mymap.lz = MyGrids[0].GSlocal[_z_];
278 mymap.am = (double)memory.all / MBYTE;
279 mymap.oh = (double)subbox.Nalloc / (double)MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask;
280 mymap.m1 = (double)memory.prods / (double)MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask;
281 mymap.m2 = (double)(memory.fields + memory.fields_to_keep) / (double)MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask;
282 mymap.m3 = (double)memory.fft / (double)MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask;
283 mymap.m4 = (double)memory.fmax_total / (double)MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask;
284 mymap.m5 = (double)memory.frag_prods / (double)MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask;
285 mymap.m6 = (double)(memory.groups + memory.frag_arrays) / (double)MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask;
286 mymap.m7 = (double)memory.frag_total / (double)MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask;
287 mymap.m8 = (double)memory.all / (double)MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask;
288
289 if (!ThisTask)
290 {
291 printf("\n");
292 printf("Map of memory usage for all MPI tasks\n");
293 printf("Task N. FFT domain mem(MB) overhead products fields ffts fmax frag pr. groups fragment total bytes per particle\n");
294 }
295 for (i = 0; i < NTasks; i++)
296 {
297 if (!ThisTask)
298 {
299 if (i)
300 MPI_Recv((void *)&mymap, sizeof(struct map), MPI_BYTE, i, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD, &status);
301 printf("%6d %4d-%4d-%4d %8.0f %6.1f %6.1f %6.1f %6.1f %6.1f %6.1f %6.1f %6.1f %6.1f\n",
302 i, mymap.lx, mymap.ly, mymap.lz, mymap.am, mymap.oh, mymap.m1, mymap.m2, mymap.m3, mymap.m4, mymap.m5, mymap.m6, mymap.m7, mymap.m8);
303 }
304 else if (ThisTask == i)
305 MPI_Send((void *)&mymap, sizeof(struct map), MPI_BYTE, 0, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
306 }
307 if (!ThisTask)
308 {
309 printf("\n");
310 fflush(stdout);
311 }
312
313 /* this is just to have a little margin */
314 memory.all += 10;
315
316 /* it tests than there is enough space to allocate all needed memory */
317 main_memory = (char *)calloc(memory.all, sizeof(char));
318 if (main_memory == 0x0)
319 {
320 printf("ERROR on task %d: I cannot allocate memory\n", ThisTask);
321 fflush(stdout);
322 return 1;
323 }
324 free(main_memory);
325
326 /* allocates main memory */
327 main_memory = (char *)calloc(memory.first_allocated, sizeof(char));
328 if (main_memory == 0x0)
329 {
330 printf("ERROR on taks %d: I cannot allocate memory after first successful attempt\n", ThisTask);
331 fflush(stdout);
332 return 1;
333 }
334
335 /* sets pointers to the allocated memory */
336 products = (product_data *)main_memory;
337 count_memory = MyGrids[0].total_local_size * sizeof(product_data);
338 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
339 for (igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
340 {
341 kdensity[igrid] = (double *)(main_memory + count_memory);
342 count_memory += MyGrids[igrid].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double);
343 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
344 }
345#ifdef TWO_LPT
346 kvector_2LPT = (double *)(main_memory + count_memory);
347 count_memory += MyGrids[0].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double);
348 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
349 source_2LPT = kvector_2LPT;
350#ifdef THREE_LPT
351 kvector_3LPT_1 = (double *)(main_memory + count_memory);
352 count_memory += MyGrids[0].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double);
353 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
354 kvector_3LPT_2 = (double *)(main_memory + count_memory);
355 count_memory += MyGrids[0].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double);
356 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
357 source_3LPT_1 = kvector_3LPT_1;
358 source_3LPT_2 = kvector_3LPT_2;
359#endif
360#endif
361 for (igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
362 {
363 for (i = 0; i < 6; i++)
364 {
365 second_derivatives[igrid][i] = (double *)(main_memory + count_memory);
366 count_memory += MyGrids[igrid].total_local_size * sizeof(double);
367 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
368 }
369 for (i = 0; i < 3; i++)
370 first_derivatives[igrid][i] = second_derivatives[igrid][i];
371 density[igrid] = second_derivatives[igrid][0];
372 }
373 // for (igrid=0; igrid<Ngrids; igrid++)
374 // {
375 // seedtable[igrid] = (unsigned int*)(main_memory + count_memory);
376 // count_memory += MyGrids[igrid].GSglobal[_x_] * MyGrids[igrid].GSglobal[_y_] * sizeof(unsigned int);
377 // }
378 //
379 /* allocates fft vectors */
380 for (igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
381 {
382 rvector_fft[igrid] = pfft_alloc_real(MyGrids[igrid].total_local_size_fft);
383 cvector_fft[igrid] = pfft_alloc_complex(MyGrids[igrid].total_local_size_fft / 2);
384 // printf("Task %d has got alignment for grid %d [ %llu %llu %llu - %llu %llu %llu ]\n",
385 // ThisTask, igrid, (unsigned long long int)rvector_fft[igrid] % 256, (unsigned long long int)rvector_fft[igrid] % 128, (unsigned long long int)rvector_fft[igrid] % 64,
386 //(unsigned long long int)cvector_fft[igrid] % 256, (unsigned long long int)cvector_fft[igrid] % 128, (unsigned long long int)cvector_fft[igrid] % 64);
387
388 if (rvector_fft[igrid] == 0x0 || cvector_fft[igrid] == 0x0)
389 {
390 printf("ERROR on taks %d: I cannot allocate memory for fft vectors\n", ThisTask);
391 fflush(stdout);
392 return 1;
393 }
394 }
395
396 if (!ThisTask)
397 printf("[%s] Memory has been successfully allocated\n", fdate());
398
399#ifdef VERBOSE
400
401 last = main_memory + memory.first_allocated;
402#ifndef TWO_LPT
403 bsize = 3 + 3 * Ngrids;
404#else
405 bsize = 4 + 3 * Ngrids;
406#endif
407 bsize *= 2;
408 bcast = (size_t *)malloc(bsize * sizeof(size_t));
409
410 n = 0;
411 bcast[n++] = (size_t)((void *)kdensity[0] - (void *)products);
412 for (igrid = 1; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
413 bcast[n++] = (size_t)((void *)kdensity[igrid] - (void *)kdensity[igrid - 1]);
414#ifdef TWO_LPT
415 bcast[n++] = (size_t)((void *)kvector_2LPT - (void *)second_derivatives[Ngrids - 1][0]);
416 bcast[n++] = (size_t)((void *)seedtable[0] - (void *)kvector_2LPT);
417#else
418 bcast[n++] = (size_t)((void *)seedtable[0] - (void *)second_derivatives[Ngrids - 1][0]);
419#endif
420 bcast[n++] = (size_t)((void *)second_derivatives[0][0] - (void *)kdensity[Ngrids - 1]);
421 for (igrid = 1; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
422 bcast[n++] = (size_t)((void *)second_derivatives[igrid][0] - (void *)second_derivatives[igrid - 1][0]);
423 /* for (igrid=1; igrid<Ngrids; igrid++) */
424 /* bcast[n++]=(size_t)((void*)seedtable[igrid]-(void*)seedtable[igrid-1]); */
425 bcast[n++] = 0;
426 bcast[n++] = (size_t)(last - (void *)seedtable[Ngrids - 1]);
427 bcast[n++] = (size_t)(last - (void *)products);
428 bcast[n++] = memory.fft;
429
430 bcast[n++] = memory.prods;
431 for (igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
432 bcast[n++] = MyGrids[igrid].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double);
433 for (igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
434 bcast[n++] = 6 * MyGrids[igrid].total_local_size * sizeof(double);
435#ifdef TWO_LPT
436#ifndef THREE_LPT
437 bcast[n++] = MyGrids[0].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double);
438#else
439 bcast[n++] = 3 * MyGrids[0].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double);
440#endif
441#endif
442 for (igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
443 bcast[n++] = MyGrids[igrid].GSglobal[_x_] * MyGrids[igrid].GSglobal[_y_] * sizeof(unsigned int);
444 bcast[n++] = count_memory;
445 bcast[n++] = memory.all;
446
447 if (!ThisTask)
448 {
449 printf("Map of memory allocations for fmax (in byte):\n");
450 printf(" TASK ");
451 printf(" PRODUCTS");
452 for (igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
453 printf(" KDENS G.%d", igrid);
454#ifdef TWO_LPT
455 printf(" KVEC 2LPT ");
456#ifdef THREE_LPT
457 printf(" KVEC 3LPT ");
458#endif
459#endif
460 for (igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
461 printf(" DERIV G.%d", igrid);
462 for (igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
463 printf(" SEEDS G.%d ", igrid);
464 printf(" MAIN MEMORY ");
465 printf("- FFT VECTORS / REQ. MEMORY\n");
466 }
467
468 for (i = 0; i < NTasks; i++)
469 {
470
471 if (!ThisTask)
472 {
473 if (i)
474 s = MPI_Recv((void *)bcast, bsize * sizeof(size_t), MPI_BYTE, i, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD, &status);
475 printf(" FOUND %5d", i);
476 for (n = 0; n < bsize / 2; n++)
477 printf(" %12zu", bcast[n]);
478 printf("\n");
479 printf(" PRED %5d", i);
480 for (n = bsize / 2; n < bsize; n++)
481 printf(" %12zu", bcast[n]);
482 printf("\n");
483 }
484 else if (ThisTask == i)
485 s = MPI_Send((void *)bcast, bsize * sizeof(size_t), MPI_BYTE, 0, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
486 }
487#endif
488
489 if (!ThisTask)
490 {
491 printf("\n");
492 fflush(stdout);
493 }
494 return 0;
495}
496
497int deallocate_fft_vectors(int ThisGrid)
498{
499
500 pfft_free(cvector_fft[ThisGrid]);
501 pfft_free(rvector_fft[ThisGrid]);
502
503 return 0;
504}
505
506int reallocate_memory_for_fragmentation()
507{
508
509 size_t count_memory, start_memory;
510 void *tmp;
511#ifdef VERBOSE
512 size_t *bcast;
513 int bsize, s, n;
514 MPI_Status status;
515 void *last;
516#endif
517
518#ifdef SNAPSHOT
519 /* initializes zacc to -1 and group_ID to 0 before redistributing it */
520 // SAREBBE DA METTERE DA UN'ALTRA PARTE
521 for (int i = 0; i < MyGrids[0].total_local_size; i++)
522 {
523 products[i].zacc = -1;
524 products[i].group_ID = 0;
525 }
526#endif
527
528 /* if the LPT sources are needed they are reassigned later */
529 for (int igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
530 kdensity[igrid] = 0x0;
531#ifdef TWO_LPT
532 kvector_2LPT = 0x0;
533#ifdef THREE_LPT
534 kvector_3LPT_1 = 0x0;
535 kvector_3LPT_2 = 0x0;
536#endif
537#endif
538
539#ifdef TWO_LPT
540 source_2LPT = 0x0;
541#ifdef THREE_LPT
542 source_3LPT_1 = 0x0;
543 source_3LPT_2 = 0x0;
544#endif
545#endif
546
547 /* these pointers are set to zero in all cases */
548 density = 0x0;
549 first_derivatives = 0x0;
550 second_derivatives = 0x0;
551 // seedtable=0x0;
552
553 /* products in the sub-box are stored in the memory that has been freed */
554
555 /* enlarge memory allocation if needed */
556 if (memory.frag_allocated > memory.first_allocated)
557 {
558 tmp = (void *)realloc(main_memory, memory.frag_allocated);
559 if (tmp != 0x0)
560 {
561 main_memory = tmp;
562 products = (product_data *)main_memory;
563 if (!ThisTask)
564 printf("[%s] Task 0 reallocated memory for %f Gb\n", fdate(), (double)memory.frag_allocated / GBYTE);
565 }
566 else
567 {
568 printf("ERROR on task %d: could not reallocate memory from %zu to %zu bytes\n", ThisTask,
569 memory.first_allocated, memory.frag_allocated);
570 fflush(stdout);
571 return 1;
572 }
573 }
574
575 /* products have already been pointed at */
576 count_memory = memory.prods;
577
578#ifdef RECOMPUTE_DISPLACEMENTS
579 /* including fields_to_keep in this case */
580 for (int igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
581 {
582 kdensity[igrid] = (double *)(main_memory + count_memory);
583 count_memory += MyGrids[igrid].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double);
584 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
585 }
586#ifdef TWO_LPT
587 kvector_2LPT = (double *)(main_memory + count_memory);
588 count_memory += MyGrids[0].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double);
589 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
590#ifdef THREE_LPT
591 kvector_3LPT_1 = (double *)(main_memory + count_memory);
592 count_memory += MyGrids[0].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double);
593 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
594 kvector_3LPT_2 = (double *)(main_memory + count_memory);
595 count_memory += MyGrids[0].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double);
596 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
597#endif
598#endif
599
600#endif
601
602 /* the frag structure follows */
603 frag = (product_data *)(main_memory + count_memory);
604 count_memory += memory.frag_prods;
605 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
606
607 start_memory = count_memory;
608
609 groups = (group_data *)(main_memory + count_memory);
610 count_memory += subbox.PredNpeaks * sizeof(group_data);
611 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
612 wheretoplace_mycat = (void *)(main_memory + count_memory);
613 count_memory += subbox.PredNpeaks * sizeof(histories_data);
614 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
615#ifdef PLC
616 plcgroups = (plcgroup_data *)(main_memory + count_memory);
617 count_memory += plc.Nmax * sizeof(plcgroup_data);
618 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
619#endif
620 group_ID = (int *)(main_memory + count_memory);
621 count_memory += subbox.Nalloc * sizeof(int);
622 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
623 linking_list = (int *)(main_memory + count_memory);
624 count_memory += subbox.Nalloc * sizeof(int);
625 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
626
627 frag_pos = (int *)(main_memory + count_memory);
628 count_memory += subbox.Nalloc * sizeof(int);
629 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
630 indices = (int *)(main_memory + count_memory);
631 count_memory += subbox.Nalloc * sizeof(int);
632 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
633 indicesY = (int *)(main_memory + count_memory);
634 count_memory += subbox.Nalloc * sizeof(int);
635 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
636 sorted_pos = (int *)(main_memory + count_memory);
637 count_memory += subbox.Nalloc * sizeof(int);
638 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
639 frag_map = (unsigned int *)(main_memory + count_memory);
640 count_memory += subbox.maplength * sizeof(unsigned int);
641 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
642 frag_map_update = (unsigned int *)(main_memory + count_memory);
643 count_memory += subbox.maplength * sizeof(unsigned int);
644 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
645
646 memset((char *)frag, 0, count_memory - start_memory);
647
648 return 0;
649}

256 This routine allocates all the memory needed to contain information

274 /* map of memory usage for all tasks */

276 mymap.ly = MyGrids[0].GSlocal[_y_];
277 mymap.lz = MyGrids[0].GSlocal[_z_];
278 mymap.am = (double)memory.all / MBYTE;
279 mymap.oh = (double)subbox.Nalloc / (double)MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask;
280 mymap.m1 = (double)memory.prods / (double)MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask;
281 mymap.m2 = (double)(memory.fields + memory.fields_to_keep) / (double)MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask;
282 mymap.m3 = (double)memory.fft / (double)MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask;
283 mymap.m4 = (double)memory.fmax_total / (double)MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask;
284 mymap.m5 = (double)memory.frag_prods / (double)MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask;
285 mymap.m6 = (double)(memory.groups + memory.frag_arrays) / (double)MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask;
286 mymap.m7 = (double)memory.frag_total / (double)MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask;
287 mymap.m8 = (double)memory.all / (double)MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask;
288
289 if (!ThisTask)
290 {
291 printf("\n");
292 printf("Map of memory usage for all MPI tasks\n");
293 printf("Task N. FFT domain mem(MB) overhead products fields ffts fmax frag pr. groups fragment total bytes per particle\n");
294 }
295 for (i = 0; i < NTasks; i++)
296 {
297 if (!ThisTask)
298 {
299 if (i)
300 MPI_Recv((void *)&mymap, sizeof(struct map), MPI_BYTE, i, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD, &status);

303 }
304 else if (ThisTask == i)
305 MPI_Send((void *)&mymap, sizeof(struct map), MPI_BYTE, 0, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
306 }
307 if (!ThisTask)
308 {
309 printf("\n");
310 fflush(stdout);
311 }
312
313 /* this is just to have a little margin */
314 memory.all += 10;
315
316 /* it tests than there is enough space to allocate all needed memory */
317 main_memory = (char *)calloc(memory.all, sizeof(char));
318 if (main_memory == 0x0)
319 {
320 printf("ERROR on task %d: I cannot allocate memory\n", ThisTask);
321 fflush(stdout);
322 return 1;
323 }
324 free(main_memory);
325
326 /* allocates main memory */
327 main_memory = (char *)calloc(memory.first_allocated, sizeof(char));
328 if (main_memory == 0x0)
329 {
330 printf("ERROR on taks %d: I cannot allocate memory after first successful attempt\n", ThisTask);
331 fflush(stdout);
332 return 1;
333 }
334
335 /* sets pointers to the allocated memory */
336 products = (product_data *)main_memory;
337 count_memory = MyGrids[0].total_local_size * sizeof(product_data);

38{
39
40 /* here it computes the size of required memory
41 and returns the number of allocatable particles for fragmentation
42
43 ZELDOVICH DISPLACEMENTS:
44
45 name sizeof N
46
47 products 4 float + 1 int = 20 MyGrids[0].total_local_size
48 kdensity 1 double = 8 MyGrids[*].total_local_size_fft
49 second_derivatives 6 double = 48 MyGrids[*].total_local_size
50
51 first_derivatives and density will share the same memory as second_derivatives
52
53 these will be allocated by pfft routines:
54
55 cvector_fft 1 double = 8 MyGrids[*].total_local_size_fft
56 rvector_fft 1 double = 8 MyGrids[*].total_local_size_fft
57
58 This is needed by GenIC:
59 seedtable 1 int = 4 MyGrids[*].GSglobal[_x_] * MyGrids[*].GSglobal[_y_]
60
61 Total for ZEL: 76 + 16 = 92 + overhead for fragmentation + smaller things (seedtable etc)
62
63 2LPT/3LPT DISPLACEMENTS
64
65 name sizeof N
66
67 products (2LPT): 7 float + 1 int = 32 MyGrids[0].total_local_size
68 products (3LPT) 13 float + 1 int = 56 MyGrids[0].total_local_size
69 kdensity 1 double = 8 MyGrids[*].total_local_size_fft
70 second_derivatives 6 double = 48 MyGrids[*].total_local_size
71 kvector_2LPT 1 double = 8 MyGrids[0].total_local_size_fft
72 kvector_3LPT_1 1 double = 8 MyGrids[0].total_local_size_fft
73 kvector_3LPT_2 1 double = 8 MyGrids[0].total_local_size_fft
74
75 first_derivatives and density will share the same memory as second_derivatives
76 source_?LPT* will share the same memory as kvector_?LPT*
77
78 This is needed by GenIC:
79 seedtable 1 int = 4 MyGrids[*].GSglobal[_x_] * MyGrids[*].GSglobal[_y_]
80
81 These will be allocated by pfft routines:
82
83 cvector_fft 1 double = 8 MyGrids[*].total_local_size_fft
84 rvector_fft 1 double = 8 MyGrids[*].total_local_size_fft
85
86 Total for 2LPT: 32 + 80 + 16 = 128 + overhead for fragmentation + smaller things
87 Total for 3LPT: 56 + 80 + 16 = 152 + overhead for fragmentation + smaller things
88
89 Timeless snapshot adds 4 bytes to the count.
90
91 In case or recomputation of displacements:
92
93 products (ZEL): 7 float + 1 int = 32 MyGrids[0].total_local_size
94 products (2LPT): 13 float + 1 int = 56 MyGrids[0].total_local_size
95 products (3LPT): 25 float + 1 int = 104 MyGrids[0].total_local_size
96 Total for ZEL: 32 + 80 + 16 = 128 + overhead for fragmentation
97 Total for 2LPT: 56 + 80 + 16 = 152 + overhead for fragmentation
98 Total for 3LPT: 104 + 80 + 16 = 200 + overhead for fragmentation
99
100 Products for fragmentation will require the same memory as those for fmax
101 plus the overhead for boundary layers.
102
103 Group catalogs will require less memory than the fmax products. This will be
104 checked with an estimate of the number of halos.
105
106 memory.prods: fmax products in fft space
107 memory.fields_to_keep LPT sources in kspace
108 memory.fields second_derivatives, // seedtable
109 memory.first_allocated: the three above
110 memory.fft: needed by pfft vectors
111 memory.fmax_total: needed by fmax, the sum of fft and first_allocated
112 memory.frag_prods: fmax products in subbox space
113 memory.frag_arrays: linking list and group ID
114 memory.groups: groups catalog, including histories, map and PLC
115 memory.frag_allocated: needed by fragment, allocated
116 memory.frag_total: needed by fragment, including FFTs
117 memory.all_allocated: total allocated memory (not including FFTs)
118 memory.all: total memory needed (including FFTs)
119
120 */
121
122 int igrid;
123 unsigned int myNalloc, Nalloc;
124
125 /***********************************************/
126 /* COLLAPSE TIMES PART */
127 /* this is the memory needed to store products */
128 memory.prods = MyGrids[0].total_local_size * sizeof(product_data); /* products */
129 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(memory.prods);
130 memory.fields = memory.fields_to_keep = 0;
131
132 /* these fields are needed after products if displacements are recomputed */
133 for (igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
134 {
135 memory.fields_to_keep += MyGrids[igrid].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double); /* kdensity */
136 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(memory.fields_to_keep);
137 }
138#ifdef TWO_LPT

146#endif
147#endif

159

171
172 /********************************************************************************/
173 /* FRAGMENTATION PART */
174 /* this is the memory needed to fragment the collapsed medium, including PLC data

176 memory.groups = subbox.PredNpeaks * sizeof(group_data);

183#endif

185 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(memory.groups);
186 memory.groups += subbox.maplength * sizeof(unsigned int);
187 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(memory.groups);
188
189 /* Here it computes the number of particles it can allocate for the subbox */
190 size_t other_mem = memory.prods + memory.groups
191#ifdef RECOMPUTE_DISPLACEMENTS
192 + memory.fields_to_keep + memory.fft
193#endif
194 ;
195
196 if (other_mem > MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask * params.MaxMemPerParticle)
197 myNalloc = 0;
198 else
199 /* -10 is to compensate for remainder in integer division */
200 myNalloc = (MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask * params.MaxMemPerParticle - other_mem) / (sizeof(product_data) + FRAGFIELDS * sizeof(int)) - 10;
201
202 /* Nalloc will be the smallest among all tasks */
203 MPI_Reduce(&myNalloc, &Nalloc, 1, MPI_UNSIGNED, MPI_MIN, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
204 MPI_Bcast(&Nalloc, 1, MPI_UNSIGNED, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
205
206 memory.frag_prods = Nalloc * sizeof(product_data);
207 memory.frag_arrays = Nalloc * FRAGFIELDS * sizeof(int);
208 /* this is the memory occupied by fragmentation */
209 memory.frag_allocated = memory.prods + memory.frag_prods + memory.groups + memory.frag_arrays
210#ifdef RECOMPUTE_DISPLACEMENTS
211 + memory.fields_to_keep
212#endif
213 ;
214 memory.frag_total = memory.frag_allocated
215#ifdef RECOMPUTE_DISPLACEMENTS
216 + memory.fft
217#endif
218 ;
219
220#ifdef CLASSIC_FRAGMENTATION
221 if (myNalloc < subbox.Npart)
222 {
223 printf("ERROR: Task %d can allocate only %d subbox particles, while %d are needed;\n",
224 ThisTask, myNalloc, subbox.Npart);
225 printf(" a large overhead is probably needed, please increase MaxMemPerParticle\n");
226 return (size_t)0;
227 }
228#endif
229
230 /* this is the largest amount of memory needed by the code */
231 memory.all_allocated = (memory.first_allocated > memory.frag_allocated ? memory.first_allocated : memory.frag_allocated);
232 memory.all = (memory.fmax_total > memory.frag_total ? memory.fmax_total : memory.frag_total);
233
234 double myfraction = (double)Nalloc / (double)subbox.Ngood;
235 double minfraction;
236 MPI_Reduce(&myfraction, &minfraction, 1, MPI_DOUBLE, MPI_MIN, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
237
238 if (!ThisTask)
239 {
240 if (minfraction < 0.5)
241 {
242 printf("ERROR: for some tasks the number of allocatable particles is only a factor %8f\n", minfraction);
243 printf(" times the number of good particles in the subbox; this is far too small!\n");
244 printf(" Please increase MaxMemPerParticle\n");
245 fflush(stdout);
246 return (size_t)0;
247 }
248 }
249
250 return Nalloc;
251}
